title: Nobel Prize in Medicine winner Susumu Tonegawa dies at 86
summary: The Japanese scientist, a pioneer in the study of the immune system, revolutionized biology with his discoveries.

title: Japan passes law penalizing destruction of national flag
summary: The rule, which punishes damage or removal of the flag, sparks debate over potential violations of freedom of expression.

title: Reform to Imperial House Law approved to ensure succession
summary: The amendment, which will allow increasing the number of members of the royal family, was approved by Parliament.

title: Government and opposition agree on new income-linked benefits system
summary: The agreement provides for the introduction of the system in fiscal year 2029, but there is still no consensus on reducing the consumption tax.

title: Japanese professional baseball teams study creating a sports lottery
summary: The proposal, based on an unpredictable random system, seeks to generate revenue for the sport.

title: Former nurse accused of injecting feces into intravenous solution at a hospital
summary: The suspect allegedly extracted fecal matter from the storage room and injected it into patients, causing their deaths.

title: US Army to conduct annual testosterone exams on its soldiers
summary: The tests aim to maintain hormone levels at a 'maximum standard' for male soldiers, and will also be applied to women.

title: Futabasha publisher recalls a book for discriminatory expressions
summary: The work 'Rupo rojo mueshi' contained descriptions that could identify individuals and generate discrimination.

title: World Cup final will be between Spain and Argentina; historic photo of the two stars goes viral
summary: The image of both players when they were young resurfaces on social media before the decisive match.

title: Former educator sentenced to 10 years in prison for stabbing a 2-year-old child
summary: The Kagoshima court considered that there was intent to kill by applying a blade to the child's neck.

title: Ex-partner rearrested for harassment in Sagamihara student murder case
summary: The suspect, already arrested for the homicide, now faces additional charges for stalking.

title: Expo president resigns over purchase of electric buses with safety risks
summary: It was pointed out that the safety risks of the vehicles were not adequately verified, leading to the resignation of the person in charge.

title: Judicial review system reform project raises doubts about transparency
summary: Experts warn that the new regulations do not guarantee full disclosure of evidence by the prosecution.

title: Hanshin Tigers win with Sato's two-run homer
summary: The Osaka team came back and defeated the Chunichi Dragons in an exciting game.

title: PayPay payment system temporarily failed; now restored
summary: Users were unable to make transactions for several minutes until the issue was resolved.

title: Seven & i plans to invest billions of yen in Poland's leading store chain
summary: The operation seeks to accelerate the group's expansion in Europe, according to close sources.

title: Fair Trade Commission admonishes seating company for improper payments
summary: The firm allegedly reduced payments to subcontractors and demanded free storage of molds.

title: Decide on Hokuriku Shinkansen extension route with a new station in Kyoto
summary: The route approved by the government and the ruling party raised doubts about the route selection.

title: Man arrested for allegedly planning a massacre in Tokyo
summary: The metropolitan police arrested the suspect for weapons possession and conspiracy to commit multiple murders.

title: Discarded cigarette butt caused a fire in Osaka that killed two people
summary: Police confirmed that the fire started from a poorly extinguished cigarette in a residential building.
